deep_dive.title: MedEvoEval: Evaluating Continual Evolution of Doctor Agents through Simulated Clinical Episodes
deep_dive.abstract: Doctor agents are moving beyond single-turn answer generation toward evolving clinical decision systems. Within an outpatient episode, they acquire evidence, use examination and consultation resources, and decide when to finalize a diagnosis and management plan. Across episodes, their behavior may change through memory, retrieval, reflection, or other update mechanisms. Current evaluations only partially cover this setting. Fixed-input medical QA benchmarks score final answers from complete inputs, whereas many interactive benchmarks still focus on individual encounters or fixed runs, providing limited support for evaluating how episode-level decisions interact with cross-episode experience. We introduce MedEvoEval, an executable longitudinal evaluation framework based on action-gated simulated outpatient episodes. Each source case is converted into role-specific patient, examination, and manager views; evidence is revealed only through valid actions; and each episode records a structured trace that links observations, actions, final outputs, manager scores, and optional experience write-back. We release a runnable E&D artifact with 700 processed episodes, provenance notes, schemas, an episode runner, scoring scripts, configurations, example logs, analysis code, and trajectory- and step-level derivatives. Experiments show that episode traces expose process costs hidden by final-answer scoring, show how MDT-style consultation reallocates resources, and support longitudinal analyses of memory maturation, held-out transfer, update-stage response, and backward retention. Together, these results show that MedEvoEval provides a concrete basis for evaluating whether doctor agents improve through experience, transfer useful behavior, and retain earlier capabilities over time.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evaluates doctor agents in simulated clinical episodes with cross-episode evolution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在模拟临床场景中评估医生智能体的持续演化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在模拟临床场景中评估医生智能体的持续演化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在模拟临床场景中评估医生智能体的持续演化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MedEvoEval is a benchmark for evaluating doctor agents across simulated clinical episodes, capturing both intra-episode decision-making and cross-episode behavioral changes through memory and reflection.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有医疗QA评测仅对完整输入评分，无法评估医生智能体在多诊次中的演化能力。本文提出MedEvoEval，通过模拟门诊病例构建可执行评测框架，支持在诊次内使用检查与资源，并在诊次间通过记忆与反思更新行为。实验表明该框架能有效捕捉智能体的持续学习与决策演化过程，为医疗智能体演化评估提供了新基准。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有医疗QA评测仅对完整输入评分，无法评估医生智能体在多诊次中的演化能力。本文提出MedEvoEval，通过模拟门诊病例构建可执行评测框架，支持在诊次内使用检查与资源，并在诊次间通过记忆与反思更新行为。实验表明该框架能有效捕捉智能体的持续学习与决策演化过程，为医疗智能体演化评估提供了新基准。

quick_skim.title: An AI agent for treatment reasoning over a biomedical tool universe
quick_skim.abstract: Treatment reasoning underpins every therapeutic decision, integrating disease context, comorbidities, medications, contraindications, and evolving biomedical knowledge to select an appropriate therapy. It is inherently iterative: candidates are weighed against many constraints, revised as evidence emerges, and grounded in verifiable sources. Here we introduce ATHENA-R1, an AI agent for treatment reasoning across all FDA approved drugs since 1939, trained by reinforcement learning over a universe of 212 biomedical tools. At each step it identifies missing information, selects and runs relevant tools, and incorporates the evidence. To train it without human-annotated traces, we build a two-level self-learning framework: multi-agent systems construct the tools, tasks, and reasoning trajectories for supervised fine-tuning, then reinforcement learning with scientific feedback rewards reasoning quality (evidence gathering, grounded tool use, logical non-redundancy). Across five benchmarks of 3,168 drug reasoning tasks and 456 patient treatment cases, ATHENA-R1 outperforms language models and tool-use systems, reaching 94.7% accuracy on open-ended drug reasoning and 82.9% on treatment reasoning, 17.8 and 10.7 points above GPT-5. In blinded evaluations by experts from 28 rare disease organizations, it is preferred over reference models on all criteria, and physicians rated it favorably on complex hospitalized cardiovascular and infectious-disease cases. Adverse-event hypotheses it generated, tested in electronic health records from 5.4 million patients, reached adjusted odds ratios of 1.48-1.84, with no elevation among negative controls. Because it requires knowing what evidence to seek before concluding, treatment reasoning has long been hard for AI; we show it can be reframed as a learnable process of iterative evidence gathering that reinforcement learning can train AI to perform.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Medical agent trained with reinforcement learning for treatment reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习训练的医疗Agent进行治疗推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习训练的医疗Agent进行治疗推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习训练的医疗Agent进行治疗推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ATHENA-R1, a medical AI agent trained with reinforcement learning over 212 biomedical tools for treatment reasoning, enabling iterative evidence gathering and self-improvement without human annotation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 治疗推理是复杂的临床决策过程。现有方法难以在缺乏人工标注轨迹的情况下训练医疗Agent。本文提出ATHENA-R1，通过强化学习在212个生物医学工具上进行训练，实现迭代式治疗推理。系统采用两级自学习框架，由多Agent构建工具链并生成训练轨迹。该Agent能覆盖所有FDA批准药物，为医疗Agent的评估提供了新的强化学习训练范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 治疗推理是复杂的临床决策过程。现有方法难以在缺乏人工标注轨迹的情况下训练医疗Agent。本文提出ATHENA-R1，通过强化学习在212个生物医学工具上进行训练，实现迭代式治疗推理。系统采用两级自学习框架，由多Agent构建工具链并生成训练轨迹。该Agent能覆盖所有FDA批准药物，为医疗Agent的评估提供了新的强化学习训练范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Reinforcement learning and LLM in medical agent evaluation

quick_skim.title: The Verbose Context Problem in Medical Records
quick_skim.abstract: The verbose context problem occurs when structured concepts have token-inefficient textual representations. This bottleneck is acute in population health: cohort-level analysis of longitudinal patient records requires reasoning over thousands of medically-coded events, often exceeding 400K tokens in total. We present PopMedQA, a benchmark isolating this problem through computational tasks on groups of longitudinal patient records. We construct the benchmark using neopatient, a new library for language-controlled generation of artificial patient records. Through extensive ablations -- including prompting strategies, prompt compression, and agentic decomposition -- we find that domain-independent methods fail to alleviate the verbose context problem. There remains significant opportunity to exploit domain-specific structure in language model inputs for population-scale reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Benchmark for medical records with agentic decomposition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于医疗记录的基准测试，包含agentic分解方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于医疗记录的基准测试，包含agentic分解方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于医疗记录的基准测试，包含agentic分解方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PopMedQA benchmark addresses the verbose context problem in longitudinal patient records, testing agentic decomposition and other methods, though not directly evaluating medical agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: PopMedQA基准测试专门针对纵向患者记录中的冗长上下文问题，通过构建人口健康级别的分析任务，测试了提示策略、提示压缩和agentic分解等方法的有效性。实验发现领域无关方法无法解决该问题，暗示需要专门针对医疗记录的特征设计方法。该基准为评估处理EHR的agent提供了重要的测试环境，但本身不直接评估agent性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: PopMedQA基准测试专门针对纵向患者记录中的冗长上下文问题，通过构建人口健康级别的分析任务，测试了提示策略、提示压缩和agentic分解等方法的有效性。实验发现领域无关方法无法解决该问题，暗示需要专门针对医疗记录的特征设计方法。该基准为评估处理EHR的agent提供了重要的测试环境，但本身不直接评估agent性能。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Benchmarks for clinical decision support agents based on EHR
